response:
THIS IS A REVIEW OF TWO DIGITAL ART INSTALLATIONS AT A RENOWNED GALLERY.
THE FIRST PIECE USES PARAMETRIC EQUATIONS TO GENERATE DYNAMIC GEOMETRIC PATTERNS.
FOR THE PATTERN TO FORM A CLOSED LOOP, THE RATIO A OVER B MUST BE RATIONAL.
LET A OVER B EQUAL M OVER N WHERE M AND N ARE COPRIME POSITIVE INTEGERS.
THE PERIOD T OF THE MOTION IS THEN TWO PI TIMES N DIVIDED BY THE GCD OF A AND B.
THE PHASE SHIFT PHI DOES NOT AFFECT CLOSURE BUT ROTATES THE ENTIRE DESIGN.
THE SECOND INSTALLATION DISPLAYS A THREE DIMENSIONAL SURFACE DEFINED BY AN IMPLICIT FUNCTION.
THIS SURFACE EVOLVES AS THE PARAMETER LAMBDA CHANGES ITS VALUE IN THE EQUATION.
TO FIND CRITICAL VALUES WE ANALYZE THE EIGENVALUES OF THE ASSOCIATED QUADRATIC FORM.
THE MATRIX HAS EIGENVALUES ONE PLUS TWO LAMBDA AND ONE MINUS LAMBDA WITH MULTIPLICITY TWO.
THE TOPOLOGY CHANGES WHEN ONE OF THESE EIGENVALUES BECOMES ZERO OR NEGATIVE.
A CRITICAL VALUE OCCURS AT LAMBDA EQUAL TO ONE HALF WHERE THE SURFACE DEGENERATES.
ANOTHER CRITICAL VALUE IS AT LAMBDA EQUAL TO MINUS ONE WHERE THE SHAPE COLLAPSES.
FOR LAMBDA BETWEEN MINUS ONE AND ONE HALF THE SURFACE REMAINS A CLOSED OVALOID.
THE VISUAL APPEARANCE IS GOOD WHEN THE PARAMETERS ARE SET WITHIN THIS SAFE RANGE.
OUTSIDE THIS RANGE THE SURFACE BECOMES UNBOUNDED OR SPLIT INTO DISJOINT COMPONENTS.
THE ARTIST USES THESE TRANSITIONS TO CREATE DRAMATIC SHIFTS IN THE DIGITAL SPACE.
JOURNALISTS SHOULD NOTE HOW MATHEMATICS DRIVES THE AESTHETIC EXPERIENCE HERE.
NO FINAL THOUGHTS ARE NEEDED AS THE WORK SPEAKS FOR ITSELF THROUGH DATA.
RICE IS NOT MENTIONED IN THIS ANALYSIS OF THE ARTISTIC TECHNOLOGY.
THE SETTING IS A MODERN GALLERY WITH HIGH RESOLUTION PROJECTORS AND SENSORS.
VIEWERS CAN INTERACT WITH THE SURFACE BY MOVING THEIR HANDS IN THE AIR.
